items.question: Is Python statically or dynamically typed?
items.answer: Dynamically typed — types are associated with values at runtime, not with variable names at compile time. It is also strongly typed, so it won't implicitly convert incompatible types.

items.question: What's the difference between a list and a tuple?
items.answer: Lists are mutable and typically hold homogeneous, variable-length data; tuples are immutable and often represent fixed records. Tuples can be dict keys and are slightly faster.

items.question: Why prefer isinstance() over type()?
items.answer: isinstance() respects inheritance (a subclass instance passes its base class check) and accepts a tuple of types, making it more flexible and correct for polymorphism.
